instruction.title: Initial Tenderizing:
steps: Wash and pat dry chicken pieces.
Apply lemon juice and salt. Keep aside for 20 minutes. This helps tenderize and remove raw odor.

instruction.title: Second Marinade:
steps: In a bowl, mix hung yogurt, cream, cashew paste, green chili paste, ginger-garlic paste, white pepper, garam masala, cardamom powder, kasuri methi, and salt.
Add chopped coriander and melted butter.
Coat the chicken thoroughly in this mixture.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4–6 hours (overnight best) for perfect restaurant-quality softness.

instruction.title: Cooking Options:
steps: Tandoor/Charcoal Grill (Best): Skewer marinated chicken and cook in a hot tandoor until golden with slight char, basting with butter-cream mixture.
Oven: Preheat to 220°C (425°F). Arrange skewered chicken on a wire rack. Cook for 12–15 minutes, basting midway. Broil for 2–3 minutes for a smoky char.
Stovetop: Heat a grill pan, brush with oil, and cook on medium-high flame until cooked, turning and basting often.

instruction.title: Finishing Touch:
steps: For a restaurant-style smoky aroma, place hot kababs in a covered bowl with a small piece of hot charcoal placed in a steel bowl inside. Add ½ tsp ghee on the coal, cover for 2 minutes.
Serve hot with mint-coriander chutney, onion rings, lemon wedges, and butter naan.
Garnish with a drizzle of cream and coriander leaves.
Use chicken thighs (juicier than breast) for tender kababs.
Always use hung curd to avoid watery marinade.
Adding cashew paste + cream gives the silky “reshmi” texture.
